summary: Iter 256 frontend verification: platform-wide iconisation ripple via ManagementShell (Refresh → IconButton), QuickDateRangeFilter (icon-only when default, brand-blue chip + label when preset selected), OperatorScopeFilter (32px Building2 icon → expands on select), plus dialog-fade preservation and FilterChipSelect tooltip suppression. All seven spec points exercised on live preview using superadmin@oryno.com. Six of seven pass cleanly; two minor polish items noted (backdrop overlay still gets animation:none because the CSS exception only excludes [role=dialog]/[data-radix-dialog-content], NOT [data-radix-dialog-overlay]; and FilterChipSelect/OperatorScopeFilter emit a React warning 'Tooltip is changing from uncontrolled to controlled' due to the open={open?false:undefined} toggle).

issues: Dialog content animates softly (animationName=fadeIn, duration=0.2s) but the backdrop overlay reports animationName=none / duration=0s because index.css L230-233 selector kills animation on every [data-state=open] that is not [role=dialog] or [data-radix-dialog-content]. The Radix overlay carries [data-radix-dialog-overlay] but no role attribute, so it is still being swept. Add :not([data-radix-dialog-overlay]) (and the matching closed-state selector) to keep the backdrop fade. Visual impact: backdrop pops on instead of fading; content still fades.

quick_date_range_filter.custom_range_calendar_opens_on_right: verified visually in popover layout (computeRange + Calendar mounted when preset==='custom')

action_items: (Minor) index.css L230-233 — extend exception list so dialog backdrop also keeps its fade: append :not([data-radix-dialog-overlay]) to the two [data-state=open]/[data-state=closed] selectors. Current state: dialog content fades softly but the backdrop pops on instantly, which slightly undermines the polish the iteration was meant to restore.

(Minor) FilterChipSelect.jsx L46 and OperatorScopeFilter.jsx L50 — pattern open={open?false:undefined} flips Radix Tooltip between controlled/uncontrolled and emits two React warnings every interaction ('Tooltip is changing from uncontrolled to controlled' + the reverse on close). Suggested fix: keep tooltip always controlled, e.g. const [tipOpen, setTipOpen] = useState(false); wire onMouseEnter/Leave + onFocus/Blur on the trigger and force tipOpen=false whenever popover open===true. No functional regression today, but it is console noise that future testers will trip on.

(Optional) When Tooltip is open AND user clicks the trigger, tooltip dismisses correctly (verified). However Radix sometimes leaves a stale tooltip behind on rapid hover→click cycles — exercise this on Bookings/Reporting where users tend to scrub presets quickly.
critical_code_review_comments: ManagementShell.jsx L100-108 — clean IconButton swap, refreshing state propagates via [&_svg]:animate-spin on disabled, label changes Refresh→Refreshing… aria-label is honoured by tooltip. Good DRY win.

QuickDateRangeFilter.jsx L103-129 — clean controlled tooltip via TooltipTrigger asChild→PopoverTrigger asChild nesting; aria-label is value-aware ('Date range' vs 'Date range: Last 7 Days'). Inline computeRange + inRange exported makes downstream filtering trivial.

OperatorScopeFilter.jsx — early return `if (!isAdmin || operators.length === 0) return null` correctly hides chip for operator role and on pages where no operators are loaded yet. Selected operator name is memoised against latest operators array — robust against late-loading lists.
index.css L224-251 — utilities layer is the right place; the :not() selector chain correctly preserves dialog content fade BUT misses the dialog overlay/backdrop case (see action item).
FilterChipSelect tooltip control pattern emits React 18 warnings — non-blocking but worth tidying.

success_rate.frontend: 6/7 spec points pass cleanly. Dialog content fade verified (content fadeIn 0.2s); backdrop fade is the one residual gap.

test_credentials: superadmin@oryno.com / testpassword123 (2-step login: 'Login to Your Account' → email + Continue → password + Login).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 256 verified via /admin/bills (mounts ManagementShell Refresh + QuickDateRangeFilter + OperatorScopeFilter on one page — best single-page smoke for all three shared components), plus /admin/operators (FilterChipSelect tooltip suppression + AdminModal dialog fade) and /management/cinema (CinemaManagement Refresh IconButton). data-testids in place: quick-date-range-trigger, date-preset-{all|today|last_7_days|...|custom|clear}, operator-scope-trigger, operator-scope-search, operator-scope-option-all, operator-scope-option-{operatorId}, filter-{label-lowercased}. Brand blue confirmed as rgb(8,44,89)=#082c59. Backdrop overlay fade is still being swept by the no-animation rule — flagged as the only residual polish item.
rca of the issue: Dialog backdrop pop-on RCA: index.css selector `[data-state="open"]:not([role="dialog"]):not([data-radix-dialog-content])` matches the Radix dialog overlay <div> (which has data-state="open" + data-radix-dialog-overlay attribute but NO role="dialog" — Radix only sets role="dialog" on the content node). So animation:none is applied to the backdrop. Fix: append :not([data-radix-dialog-overlay]) to both the open and closed selectors at L230-231. Reproduction: visit /admin/operators logged in as superadmin, click any Eye icon on an operator row, observe computed style of div[data-radix-dialog-overlay] → animationName=none/animationDuration=0s while the content div[role=dialog] shows fadeIn / 0.2s.
